sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare previous and current frames to see what changed
prev_seg = previous_frame.segmentation['nodes']
curr_seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']

print("=== PREVIOUS FRAME ===")
for n in prev_seg:
    if n['pixels'] <= 10:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

print("\n=== CURRENT FRAME ===")
for n in curr_seg:
    if n['pixels'] <= 10:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check what the white cross area looks like now
print("\n=== Checking if white cross moved ===")
prev_white = [n for n in prev_seg if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] == 5]
curr_white = [n for n in curr_seg if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] == 5]
print(f"Previous white cross: {prev_white}")
print(f"Current white cross: {curr_white}")

# Check purple squares
prev_purple = [n for n in prev_seg if n['color'] == 'p']
curr_purple = [n for n in curr_seg if n['color'] == 'p']
print(f"\nPrevious purple: {[(n['id'], n['boundary']) for n in prev_purple]}")
print(f"Current purple: {[(n['id'], n['boundary']) for n in curr_purple]}")

# Check gray dots
prev_gray = [n for n in prev_seg if n['color'] == 'G' and n['pixels'] == 1]
curr_gray = [n for n in curr_seg if n['color'] == 'G' and n['pixels'] == 1]
print(f"\nPrevious gray dots count: {len(prev_gray)}")
print(f"Current gray dots count: {len(curr_gray)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
